text: The Coliseum squats at the city's north end like a blunt fist of stone. The outer walls are old — older than most of the city around it — and newer mortar fills cracks that open year after year. Inside, tiered benches ring a sanded pit. The crowd here is never entirely sober and never entirely sane, but they know a good fight when they see one.
Distant shouting rises and falls from inside. Someone is winning something.
